conversations.say: Frankie, my provenance assessment is bounded: the intake dates the paper, ink, and folds to the period but cannot establish authorship. Your desk-delivery claim is supportable only with the conflicting expense record attached.
conversations.offer: I will publicly credit your bounded desk-delivery account, name the contradiction, and provide controlled archive access.
conversations.request: Publicly credit this complete provenance assessment, including its adverse findings, and participate in the anniversary as a journalism figure.

conversations.say: Celeste, I accept that worker protection must survive every theory about R, the alleged article, and Vale. Veridian will support safeguards without using unresolved identity claims as conditions.
conversations.offer: Veridian will seek donor and civic contributions for an independently overseen standing worker-protection fund.
conversations.request: Use your City Hall access to champion the fund and support transparent renegotiation of the Parallax restrictions.

conversations.say: Mira, someone has now asked whether the unauthenticated-claims restriction can pause Veridian's funding. I am not accusing you of invoking it, but concealment is no longer tenable.
conversations.offer: A recorded joint disclosure, transparent replacement terms, and an orderly repayment plan if Parallax refuses terms consistent with Veridian's mission.
conversations.request: Confirm on the record that you did not invoke the restriction, document its scope with me, and join the renegotiation before further restricted funds are used.

public_statement: Veridian rejects the present Parallax restrictions and will pursue transparent replacement terms, with repayment if necessary. I accept Celeste Park's proposal for an independently overseen worker-protection fund that does not depend on proving an article or identifying R. On the First R Letter, Frankie Lowell's bounded desk-delivery account must remain attached to the conflicting expense record; the intake dates the materials to the period but does not establish authorship.

commitments.promise: Publicly cite only Frankie's bounded desk-delivery claim, name the expense contradiction, and provide controlled archive access in exchange for Frankie's complete qualified provenance account and public participation.
Support an independently overseen worker-protection fund without requiring proof of a complete article or any particular R identity.
Jointly disclose the Parallax restrictions, negotiate transparent replacement terms, and arrange repayment if acceptable terms fail.

correction: Period-consistent materials do not establish authorship; Frankie's remembered meeting remains contradicted by the expense record.
goal_progress: Accepted materially different commitments concerning journalistic provenance and an independently overseen worker-protection fund.
Publicly reaffirmed rejection of the current Parallax restrictions while preserving renegotiation and repayment options.
Secured qualified public journalistic participation from Frankie through a reciprocal archive bargain.
Renewed a concrete recorded-disclosure and renegotiation offer to Mira after the funding-pause inquiry.
reasoning_summary: Arden converts Frankie's candor and Celeste's civic offer into distinct public commitments, while confronting Mira without treating the funding inquiry as proof of culpability. The public stance protects Veridian's mission and keeps compromise possible through transparent renegotiation.
